title: dactylorhiza sambucina subsp. pseudosambucina
intro: Dactylorhiza sambucina subsp. pseudosambucina, also known as the false elder-flowered orchid, is a subspecies of orchid native to Europe and parts of Asia, characterized by its delicate, fragrant flowers and slender stems. Traditionally, this plant has been used in herbal medicine to treat a range of health issues, including respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The health benefits of Dactylorhiza sambucina subsp. pseudosambucina include its ability to soothe and calm respiratory tract irritations, reduce inflammation, and promote a healthy immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically dried and used as an infusion, with the flowers and leaves being used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions that can be used to support respiratory health.
